purpose: Promote additional theory and device-facing MoTe2 papers that sharpen higher-band, multilayer, and superconducting-junction context.

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; useful model paper for second flat Chern band and twist-angle transferability.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: second flat Chern band
nodes.summary: A second topological flat-band regime that can emerge at lower twist angle and broaden the range of correlated phases available in twisted TMD homobilayers.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: interface-bulk stratification
nodes.summary: In multilayer twisted MoTe2, moire-interface layers can remain strongly reconstructed while outer layers relax toward bulk-like behavior, causing electronic isolation and band reordering.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: A transferable continuum model predicts a second flat Chern band near 2 degrees in twisted MoTe2.
aliases: second flat Chern band near 2 degrees
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv theory says a twist-angle-transferable continuum model captures tMoTe2 and tWSe2 across angles and predicts a second flat Chern band near 2 degrees when interlayer potential difference becomes comparable to interlayer tunneling.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Twisted multilayer MoTe2 can separate moire-interface and bulk-like layers, producing band reordering and weak-gate-tunable Chern motifs.
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv paper reports strongest interface-bulk stratification at intermediate angles around 2 to 5 degrees, suppressed interface-bulk hybridization, and in twisted double bilayer MoTe2 a coexistence of honeycomb and triangular frontier-band motifs whose relative ordering can be shifted by twist angle and weak gating.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Pd-metalized twisted MoTe2 nanojunctions show anomalous superconducting behavior that may involve mixed even- and odd-parity pairing.
aliases: V-shaped Ic minimum at zero field
nodes.summary: The 2024 arXiv device paper reports robust superconducting nanojunctions across about 20 moire cells with a higher-than-expected critical-field scale and a V-shaped zero-field critical-current minimum, arguing these features are absent in natural bilayer junctions and may reflect mixed-parity superconductivity.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Can engineered twisted MoTe2 nanojunctions become a controlled probe of FCI-adjacent superconductivity?
nodes.summary: The device paper presents a pathway to engineer and investigate superconductivity in fractional Chern insulator settings, but the relation between the observed junction anomalies and intrinsic moire superconductivity remains unresolved.

provenance.method: Codex curation inference from arXiv abstract
